title: Add site quality gates: link check, a11y, lighthouse, search
description:
Site lacks automated quality gates. No link checker so reference cross-links can rot. No accessibility check so theme regressions go unnoticed. No search so docs are unbrowseable as content grows. No performance budget despite open chunk-size issue (sideshowdb-ivp).

EARS:
- The Sideshowdb site CI shall fail if any internal link in the assembled Pages artifact is broken.
- The Sideshowdb site CI shall fail if axe-core (or equivalent) reports any serious or critical accessibility violation on Home, Docs, Playground, Reference.
- The Sideshowdb site CI shall record a Lighthouse score for the homepage and shall fail when Performance, Accessibility, or Best Practices fall below a configured threshold.
- The Sideshowdb site shall provide full-text search across authored docs (Pagefind or equivalent static index).
- Where reference docs link to authored concepts, the link checker shall validate both directions.

Acceptance:
- New CI step runs link checker against dist/pages and blocks on broken links
- New CI step runs axe + lighthouse against built site
- Pagefind (or equivalent) integrated, searchable in browser, indexed at build time
- Thresholds documented in scripts/ or workflow
acceptance_criteria: CI runs link check + axe + lighthouse against assembled artifact with documented thresholds. Pagefind index built and search UI live. Broken links / a11y violations / score regressions fail CI.

title: Wire WASM into playground tour mode that narrates Git→Sideshowdb mapping
description:
Current playground fetches refs and shows a static blurb. WASM module is loaded but never consumes fetched data. Spec requires guided inspection through small set of high-value views with explanatory copy that maps GitHub/Git data to Sideshowdb concepts. Playground today does not deliver that.

EARS:
- When a user loads a repo in the playground, the Sideshowdb site shall pass fetched ref data to the loaded WASM module for projection.
- When the WASM module returns a projection, the Sideshowdb site shall render at least one Sideshowdb-derived view alongside the raw GitHub view.
- The Sideshowdb playground shall present a stepwise tour with at least three narration steps mapping repository structures to Sideshowdb concepts.
- If the WASM module fails to load or projection fails, then the Sideshowdb site shall continue to render the source data view and shall explain that derived views are unavailable.

Acceptance:
- Playground calls a WASM-exported projection function on real fetched ref data
- Side-by-side source vs derived view renders for sample repo
- Tour steps render and advance via UI control
- Fallback path tested when WASM load fails
acceptance_criteria: Playground passes fetched ref data to WASM, renders derived view side by side with source, exposes 3+ tour steps, falls back gracefully on WASM failure. Tests cover happy + WASM-failure paths.

title: Expand authored docs beyond stub pages
description:
site/src/routes/docs currently has three near-empty stubs (getting-started, concepts, playground). Spec calls for install/quickstart, conceptual guides for the Git-backed model, walkthroughs that connect repo data to Sideshowdb concepts, examples, tutorials, and links into the generated reference. Current pages do not meet that bar.

EARS:
- The Sideshowdb docs section shall provide a Getting Started page with installable Zig 0.16 prerequisites, build commands, and at least one verifiable end-to-end example.
- The Sideshowdb docs section shall provide an Architecture page that explains events, refs, projections, and local-first operation as derived views over Git.
- The Sideshowdb docs section shall provide a Projection Model walkthrough mapping a real public repo to Sideshowdb concepts.
- The Sideshowdb docs section shall provide a Concept-to-Reference cross-link for each concept page, pointing to the relevant generated /reference/ symbol.
- Where a docs page describes user-facing behavior, the page shall cite the EARS requirement it documents.

Acceptance:
- Getting Started, Architecture, Concepts, Projection Walkthrough, Playground Guide exist with non-stub content
- Each concept links into /reference/ for at least one anchor symbol
- Sidebar navigation reflects new structure
- bun run check + bun run test pass
acceptance_criteria: Five authored docs pages with real content (getting-started, architecture, concepts, projection walkthrough, playground guide). Each links into /reference/. Sidebar updated. Tests pass.

title: Add Zig doc-comments with runnable examples across src/core
description:
Autodoc reference page is sparse because public symbols in src/core/*.zig lack /// doc-comments and example blocks. Authoritative low-level reference must include intent, parameters, errors, and at least one example per public symbol so the generated /reference/ page meets a usable docs bar.

EARS:
- The Sideshowdb reference docs shall provide a documentation comment for every public declaration in src/core/*.zig.
- Where a public function can fail, the reference docs shall enumerate each error variant with a plain-language explanation.
- Where a public function performs non-trivial work, the reference docs shall include at least one runnable code example exercised by an existing test.
- If a public declaration is intentionally undocumented (internal-only re-export), then the source shall mark it with an explicit rationale comment instead of leaving it bare.

Acceptance:
- zig build docs (or scripts/build-zig-docs.sh) produces /reference/ with no public symbol missing a doc-comment
- Examples in doc-comments are referenced by, or copied verbatim from, real test code so they cannot rot silently
- Lint script (added by this issue) fails CI when an undocumented public decl is added
acceptance_criteria: zig build docs produces reference page where every public src/core/*.zig declaration has doc-comment + error enumeration + at least one example. CI lint blocks undocumented public decls.

title: Design GitHub Pages site for docs, product, and playground
description: Create a design for a GitHub Pages site that combines product marketing, authored documentation, generated Zig reference docs, and a browser-only playground for exploring public GitHub repositories through the sideshowdb model.
design: Use a SveltePress site shell with Graph Atlas visual direction, hybrid documentation model, Zig-generated reference docs, and a browser-only read-only playground for public GitHub repositories.
acceptance_criteria: A written design spec defines the site architecture, information architecture, visual direction, GitHub Pages deployment flow, public-repo-only playground behavior, and testing boundaries.

title: Add release workflow for tag-triggered binary publishing
description:
Add .github/workflows/release.yml that triggers on git tag push (v*) and produces release artifacts: CLI binaries for linux/macos/windows on amd64+arm64 (6 binaries) plus the wasm32-freestanding artifact. Cross-compile from a single ubuntu-latest runner using zig. Publish all artifacts to a GitHub Release.

Acceptance:
- Workflow triggers on tags matching v*
- Produces 6 CLI archives (linux-amd64, linux-arm64, macos-amd64, macos-arm64, windows-amd64, windows-arm64)
- Produces sideshowdb.wasm
- Creates GitHub release attached with all artifacts
- Uses Zig 0.16.0 to match CI
close_reason: Release workflow merged in #5 and validated by tag v0.0.1-alpha01: 6 CLI archives + wasm + SHA256SUMS published; ubi install verified end-to-end on macos-arm64.

title: Fix PR CI and split workflow jobs
description: Bring the CI workflow split and caching improvements onto the document PR branch, fix formatting drift reported by GitHub Actions, and push until PR #3 is green.

title: Implement namespaced versioned document read/write slice
description: Implement the first public document slice over Git refs: add a namespaced, versioned DocumentStore over RefStore, expose doc put/get via the CLI and host-backed WASM surface, add EARS requirements, and cover latest/versioned retrieval with tests.

title: MVP: git ref storage abstraction + impl
description: Implement first concept from spec — controlled-section storage backed by a git ref. Add a RefStore trait-style abstraction (zig vtable pattern modeled on std.mem.Allocator) plus a GitRefStore implementation that drives git plumbing (hash-object, mktree/write-tree, commit-tree, update-ref) via subprocess. Ship a spec doc explaining the storage model and embedding a zig-trait-vs-Scala-3-trait primer for the project author. Tests run against a real ephemeral git repo.
design: Ref namespace refs/sideshowdb/<section>; tree under ref holds blobs keyed by path. Writes use a private temp index file so we never touch the working index. Subprocess-based for MVP — libgit2/zig-native parsing is later work.
acceptance_criteria: Spec doc lands at docs/development/specs/git-ref-storage-spec.md; RefStore + GitRefStore compile; integration test creates temp repo, puts a key, reads it back, lists keys, deletes, and verifies via raw git plumbing; zig build / zig build test green
close_reason: RefStore abstraction + GitRefStore impl shipped. Spec at docs/development/specs/git-ref-storage-spec.md includes Zig-vs-Scala-3 trait primer. 9/9 tests pass including end-to-end git plumbing roundtrip.

title: Bootstrap multi-module zig project skeleton
description: Initial scaffolding for sideshowdb in zig 0.16. Provide core library module, native CLI executable, and wasm32-freestanding library target. No spec implementation yet — only build wiring, module graph, hello-world entrypoints, and tests scaffolding.
design: Single build.zig defines: (1) core module exported via b.addModule; (2) cli exe linking core; (3) wasm static lib with wasm32-freestanding target linking core; (4) test step covering core
acceptance_criteria: zig build succeeds; zig build test passes; zig build wasm produces wasm artifact; zig build run prints sideshowdb banner
close_reason: Multi-module skeleton landed: core lib, native CLI, wasm32-freestanding client, integration tests. zig build / test / wasm / run all green.

title: Expand WASM browser bridge beyond runtime metadata
description: The current browser wrapper only reads banner/version metadata from the shipped Zig WASM artifact. Follow up with a safer host bridge for document get/put or projection reads, including an explicit request-buffer strategy and clearer error signaling.

title: Reduce GitHub Pages site chunk size
description: The site build currently emits a >500 kB client chunk warning from Vite/SveltePress. Investigate chunk splitting, route-level code loading, or manual chunking so the first-release Pages site has a healthier client bundle.

title: Split CI format and link checks into separate jobs
description: Adjust the GitHub Actions CI workflow so formatting and link validation run in their own jobs instead of being bundled into a single job.
close_reason: Completed by PR #3 under follow-up task sideshowdb-kin

title: Extend document slice with list/delete/history operations
description: Follow the initial namespaced, versioned document read/write slice by adding document list, delete, and version-history traversal semantics across DocumentStore, CLI, and WASM.

title: Evaluate ziggit backend and host integration for document refs
description: Prototype or evaluate a non-subprocess document ref backend using ziggit and tighten the WASM host integration contract for version-aware latest reads and writes.

title: Address PR feedback on VersionId alias
description: Respond to PR #3 feedback by introducing a VersionId alias in the RefStore seam and propagating it through implementations without changing behavior.

title: Deduplicate CLI usage handling
description: Refactor the CLI app to use a shared usage message/helper and ensure malformed argument paths return the same usage failure output.
